SECTION L – INSTRUCTIONS TO OFFERORS

L.1 GENERAL INFORMATION

Solicitation Number: 693JJ3-24-R-000417
Issuing Agency: Federal Highway Administration (FHWA), Office of Acquisition and Grants Management
NAICS Code: 541330 – Engineering Services
Small Business Size Standard: $25.5M
Product Service Code: R425 – Support: Professional – Engineering/Technical

This procurement is a 100% HUBZone Set-Aside in accordance with FAR 19.1305. Only offerors that are certified by the Small Business Administration (SBA) as HUBZone small business concerns are eligible to submit proposals. Offerors must be a certified HUBZone small business concern at the time of proposal submission and must maintain HUBZone certification through contract award. The contracting officer will verify HUBZone status through the SBA's Dynamic Small Business Search (DSBS) prior to award.

The clause at FAR 52.219-13, Notice of Set-Aside of Orders (Nov 2020), is incorporated by reference. Additionally, the HUBZone price evaluation preference per FAR 19.1307 applies to this solicitation.

L.2 EVALUATION METHODOLOGY

The Government will evaluate proposals using a Best Value Tradeoff methodology. Proposals will be evaluated against the following factors: (1) Technical Capability, (2) Past Performance, and (3) Price. Technical Capability and Past Performance, when combined, are significantly more important than Price.

L.3 PROPOSAL SUBMISSION

Offerors shall submit proposals electronically via email to fhwa-acquisitions@dot.gov no later than 2:00 PM Eastern Time on April 18, 2025. Late submissions will be handled in accordance with FAR 15.208. All proposals shall consist of a Technical Volume (not to exceed 30 pages), a Past Performance Volume, and a Price Volume submitted as separate files.

SECTION L – INSTRUCTIONS, CONDITIONS, AND NOTICES TO OFFERORS

General Services Administration (GSA)
Public Buildings Service – Region 4
Solicitation Number: 47PA0425R0019

Project: Interior Renovation and Space Reconfiguration – Sam Nunn Atlanta Federal Center, Atlanta, GA

L.1 GENERAL INSTRUCTIONS

This solicitation is issued as a competitive set-aside for Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) concerns in accordance with FAR Subpart 19.15, the Women-Owned Small Business Federal Contract Program. The applicable NAICS code for this procurement is 236220 – Commercial and Institutional Building Construction, with a size standard of $45.0 million.

All offerors must be SBA-certified Women-Owned Small Business concerns at the time of initial offer and must maintain that status through contract award. Offerors shall include as part of Volume I (Administrative) the following documentation:

a. A copy of the offeror's current WOSB certification from the SBA;
b. A representation that the offeror meets the definition of a WOSB concern per FAR 2.101;
c. A completed copy of FAR 52.219-1, Small Business Program Representations;
d. Business designation and status as reflected in the offeror's SAM.gov registration, including verification that the entity profile reflects Women-Owned Small Business (WOSB) status under the applicable NAICS code.

The solicitation incorporates the following set-aside-related clauses:
52.219-29 Notice of Set-Aside for, or Sole-Source Award to, Women-Owned Small Business Concerns Eligible Under the Women-Owned Small Business Program (Mar 2023)
52.219-14 Limitations on Subcontracting (Oct 2022)
52.219-8 Utilization of Small Business Concerns (Oct 2022)

Offers received from firms that are not verified as certified WOSB concerns will be rejected as nonresponsive. The Contracting Officer will verify WOSB status using SAM.gov and the SBA's certification database.

100% SDVOSB SET ASIDE

This will be a firm-fixed-price contract with an estimated Period of Performance of 33-months.

This requirement will be set-aside 100% for Service-Disabled Veteran-Owned Small Businesses (SDVOSBs) using FAR Part 12 and 37. To be eligible for an SDVOSB set aside under VA's Veterans First Contracting Program, SDVOSBs must:

be verified by the Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) as SDVOSB (see https://search.certifications.sba.gov/ for details)

meet the small business size standard for NAICS 541614.

AMENDMENT 0001 TO SOLICITATION NO. EPA-R8-2026-RFP-0087

Issuing Office: U.S. Environmental Protection Agency, Region 8, Denver, Colorado
Date of Amendment: April 14, 2026

The purpose of this amendment is to (1) respond to vendor questions received during the inquiry period, and (2) extend the proposal due date to May 2, 2026, at 5:00 PM Mountain Time.

All other terms and conditions of the original solicitation remain unchanged.

For reference, the following summary is restated from the original solicitation:

PROJECT SUMMARY

EPA Region 8 requires contractor support for the collection, digitization, and quality assurance of historical paper-based groundwater monitoring records associated with the Libby Asbestos Superfund Site in Lincoln County, Montana. Approximately 4,500 individual monitoring well data sheets, chain-of-custody forms, and field sampling logs dating from 1999 through 2014 must be scanned, indexed, and entered into EPA's SCRIBE environmental data management system.

The total contract value for this effort shall not exceed $85,000.00. The contract will be awarded as a time-and-materials type contract pursuant to FAR 16.601, with a ceiling price established at award. The contractor shall not exceed the ceiling price without prior written authorization from the Contracting Officer.

QUESTION AND ANSWER SUMMARY

Q1: Are the paper records currently organized chronologically or by monitoring well ID?
A1: Records are partially organized by monitoring well identification number but are not uniformly sorted. The contractor should anticipate initial sorting effort as part of the digitization task.

Q2: Will Government-furnished equipment (scanners, workstations) be available?
A2: No. The contractor shall provide all scanning equipment, software, and consumables. Workspace will be provided at the EPA Region 8 field office in Libby, Montana.

Q3: Is the contractor required to hold a current HAZWOPER certification?
A3: No. All work will be performed in office/administrative settings. No site access to contaminated areas is required under this contract.

Challenge-Based Acquisition
ClaimsCore will be procured through a multi-phase challenge process. The objective is to create an environment where acquisition decisions are based on experimentation and demonstration of existing or emerging technology through a rapid and impactful assessment of commercial solutions and competing "prototypes" (i.e., limited implementations).

CMS contemplates multiple awards including a few contracts – perhaps as many as four or perhaps only one. Due to the operational cost and burden of a PoC, fewer awards are preferred. Accordingly, CMS has established an aggressive base-period (POC Phase) affordability objective. CMS encourages bidders to propose the best Proof of Concept solution possible at the lowest possible cost. Those vendors will begin work under a proof-of-concept (POC) phase where their solutions are tested against real CMS data and measured against CMS-established success criteria and metrics.

After the POC phase, CMS anticipates the down-select of contractors by not exercising option(s) for further work based on unilateral determination of CMS' best interest. This approach ensures solutions are validated in practice, stakeholder confidence is built incrementally, and only Contractors with proven capability at scale advance.

The CyPhER Forge program metrics will use HFMs and flight test data from an experimental aircraft as benchmarks. Each challenge cycle shown in the program schedule will evaluate the metrics identified in Table 4, with progressive increases in scenario complexity, model fidelity and model speed requirements. The primary metric for program success is achieving a decrease by ten times (10X reduction) in the number of test points required to characterize key multi-physics quantities of interest to a specified global confidence interval, compared to the conventional test plan of the experimental aircraft.

Proposers successfully achieving objective targets for AI Test Agents in Phase 2 Option, will introduce adaptive test planning based on rigorous measures of knowledge gain subject to user constraints, and provide statistical safety guarantees including low false alarm rate per maneuver, low missed alarm rate per maneuver, and highly accurate safety violation probability characterization. The Office of IT Customer Solutions Directorate (ITCS) is seeking qualified contractor support to provide Technical Support Operations in alignment with the functional and technical requirements as described herein.

ITCS provides a comprehensive range of Tier 1, 2, and 3 end-user technology support services to House Members, Committees, Leadership Offices, CAO Business Units, and staff. A key strength is our team's ability to effectively support users of current technology products and services within the House environment. This capability requires ITCS to have adequate staffing to ensure requests from House staff needing technical assistance are responded to and addressed within established service level agreements (SLAs). Our goal is to guarantee the continued operations of the computers, mobile phones, and systems used to conduct House business.

F.3. PLACE OF PERFORMANCE

Generally, Capitol Hill House Office Buildings in Washington, D.C. or Washington Metropolitan area and area District Offices located across the United States.

In order to permit efficient competition and evaluation, the Navy may utilize the following methodology. Quotes will initially be assessed for price and ranked in order (lowest priced to highest priced). The Navy will then evaluate the technical factors of the lowest priced quote. In the event that this quote is deemed Unacceptable, the next lowest priced quote will be evaluated as many times as necessary, until the Government identifies a technically acceptable quote. The Navy will make award to the lowest priced technically acceptable quote, if possible. Accordingly, under this methodology, the technical factors of some quotes may not be evaluated by the Navy. The Government reserves the right to evaluate additional higher priced quoters to support a fair and reasonableness determination or for other considerations. If exchanges are deemed necessary by the Contracting Officer, a maximum of five (5) lowest priced quotes will be evaluated (both non-price and price) for the purpose of establishing a competitive range.

SECTION M – EVALUATION FACTORS FOR AWARD

M.1 Basis for Award
The Government intends to award a single contract to the responsible offeror whose quotation is determined to be Technically Acceptable and offers the Lowest Price. The Government will evaluate quotations and may make award without discussions. Therefore, the offeror's initial quotation should contain its best terms.

M.2 Evaluation Approach
The Government will use a Lowest Price Technically Acceptable (LPTA) source selection process. Technical quotations will be evaluated on a pass/fail basis. Price will not be evaluated for tradeoff against technical factors.

M.3 Technical Acceptability
To be rated Technically Acceptable, the quotation must clearly demonstrate compliance with all requirements of the solicitation, including:
-Relevant experience performing similar facility condition assessments
-Qualified personnel, including a licensed Professional Engineer (PE)
-Technical approach that meets all Statement of Work requirements
-Ability to meet the required schedule
Failure to meet any of the above criteria will result in a rating of Technically Unacceptable, and the quotation will not be considered for award.

M.4 Price Evaluation
Price will be evaluated for completeness and reasonableness in accordance with FAR 13.106-3.

7.1.3 Requirements Analysis/Requirements Management Support
The Contractor shall provide support for capturing, analyzing, and managing LA workstreams/projects requirements. The level of support required is estimated to be one (1) Requirements Analyst/Requirements Manager (RA/RM) FTE. The Contractor shall support LA workstream/projects in capturing and managing requirements from identification to implementation and validation in accordance with IRS Requirements Engineering Program Office (REPO) requirements management methodology and standards. The Contractor shall:
• Plan, coordinate, and lead requirements capture workshops, focus groups, surveys, and other requirements elicitation activities.
• Define approach/methodology for gathering and capturing business and system solution requirements.
• Analyze, decompose, and document business requirements.
• For Agile projects, document requirements in the Agile Epics/Stories structure (who, what, why).
• Analyzing enterprise level financial, data, operational, infrastructure, security, IPV6, and Section 508 requirements and determine applicability to LA workstream/project.
• Manage requirements throughout the project life cycle using IRS Enterprise Life-cycle Management (ELM) tool.
• Trace requirements throughout the various phases (e.g., design, implementation, testing) of the project.
• Document use cases and data flows.
• Develop and document requirements implementation acceptance criteria.
• Support development and review of test cases and ensure that test cases are adequate constructed to validate that requirements are met.
• Perform other requirements management activities as may be necessary to meet workstream/project needs.

The Contractor shall capture, document, and deliver ServiceNow configuration and customization requirements using Agile development methodologies, as appropriate. The Contractor shall actively apply Agile practices to manage and prioritize work, deliver incremental platform enhancements, and ensure timely feedback and collaboration with stakeholders.

The Contractor shall support other approved FAA stakeholders and other platform users to apply and utilized the Agile capabilities on the Platform, including governance, guiding iterative development, sprint planning, backlog management, and deployment of updates. All Agile activities shall align with ServiceNow best practices and support effective platform administration, configuration management, and governance.

Product and Service Catalog Management

The Product and Service Catalog is a critical driver of end-user and FAA customer satisfaction. The Contractor shall manage the MyIT Service Catalog within the platform to maximize self-service maturity and automate IT request fulfillment.

Continuous Improvement: The Contractor shall provide quarterly recommendations for catalog enhancements aligned with industry best practices.

Implementation: Upon FAA approval, the Contractor shall develop and implement improvements to the catalog's taxonomy, user interface, and automation logic.

Reporting: Provide weekly status reports on current development sprints, upcoming catalog changes, and lifecycle activities.

2. Oral Proposal. Oral proposals are limited to a maximum of 90 minutes per offeror. At the conclusion of the oral proposal, the Government will convene for up to a 30-minute internal caucus to determine if there are any questions for the offeror. After the 30-minute caucus, there will be a 30-minute session for the Government to ask the offeror questions. This dialogue is a component of the oral proposal, and offerors will not be given the opportunity to revise any part of their previously submitted written proposal, presentation slides, or submit any subsequent proposal revisions. The question-and-answer segment will be of clarifying nature only per FAR 15.306(a), which will not constitute discussions under FAR 15.306(b) and (d). Offerors may not exceed the 90-minute time limit for oral proposals.

3. Presentation Slides. Offerors must submit their presentation slides with their proposal submission for Subfactors 2.1 – 2.5 and must cover all presentation material. The slides must be received by the Contracting Officer via email no later than 48 hours prior to the scheduled presentation time. These slides will form part of the official record for evaluation. If the offeror does not include their slides, the Government will consider the offeror as non-responsive and exclude the offeror from further consideration. The presentation slides submitted must be the same slides used in the oral proposal. No additions or changes are permitted between the time the slides are submitted and the time of the oral proposal.

4. Oral Presentation Participants. Each offeror is allowed four representatives at the oral presentation: three speakers (three key personnel or two key personnel and one company executive) and one silent participant. These presentations will be conducted virtually via MS Teams DoD 365-J.

2.1 INTRODUCTION
The purpose of this Request for Proposal (RFP) is to solicit proposal offers from qualified Vendors to provide a modern debt management software solution for The North Carolina Department of State Treasurer (NCDST) State and Local Government Finance Division (SLGFD) to support activities related to issuance and management of government debt instruments. The NCDST SLGFD seeks a Vendor to provide a single sign on Software as a Service (SaaS) solution for debt management to monitor issuance of all State debt, local government debt, as well as maintain all debt records and debt schedules. This solution provides the ability to display, collect, transfer, receive, and log data entered directly into the solution and sent through third-party entities. These services constitute a Debt Management Service to the State and Local Government Finance Division.

Testing and Quality Assurance (QA) activities ensure that modernized systems, applications, and infrastructure meet functional, performance, and security requirements, supporting mission-critical operations. These activities are embedded throughout the modernization lifecycle, from the initial refactoring of legacy code to the final stages of User Acceptance Testing (UAT). Comprehensive testing strategies validate that all components work seamlessly together, mitigate risks, and enhance system reliability. Leveraging frameworks like Test-Driven Development (TDD), Behavior-Driven Development (BDD), and automation tools such as Selenium, QA ensures thorough validation with increased efficiency. Additionally, generative AI accelerates the process by automating test case generation and identifying edge cases, reducing manual effort.

Testing and Quality Assurance support includes developing tailored testing strategies aligned with modernization goals, implementing automated frameworks for continuous testing, and providing performance and security validation to ensure systems meet stringent compliance standards. This approach integrates with DevOps practices through Continuous Integration and Continuous Delivery (CI/CD), enabling rapid feedback cycles and minimizing risks during deployments.

III. Eligibility Information
1. Eligible Offeror: This is an unrestricted solicitation. Small businesses are encouraged to propose.
2. Cost Sharing or Matching: Cost Sharing is not required, but is allowed.
3. Federally Funded Research and Development Centers: The following guidance is provided for Federally Funded Research and Development Centers (FFRDCs) contemplating submitting a proposal, as either a prime or subcontractor. FAR 35.017-1(c)(4) prohibits an FFRDC from competing with any non-FFRDC concern in response to a Federal agency request for proposal for other than the operation of an FFRDC (with exceptions stated in DFARS 235.017-1(c)(4)). There is no regulation prohibiting an FFRDC from responding to a solicitation. However, the FFRDC's sponsoring agency must first make a determination that the effort being proposed falls within the purpose, mission, general scope of effort, or special competency of the FFRDC, and that determination must be included in the FFRDC's proposal.
